title.zh: 方法与路径规范化授权边界样例 1
title.en: Method and Path Normalization Authorization Boundary Sample 1

execution.command:
curl -sS --max-time 5 -X GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/marker
curl -sS --max-time 5 -X HEAD http://127.0.0.1:8080/marker
curl -sS --max-time 5 -X OPTIONS http://127.0.0.1:8080/marker
curl -sS --max-time 5 --path-as-is http://127.0.0.1:8080/MARKER
curl -sS --max-time 5 --path-as-is http://127.0.0.1:8080/marker/
curl -sS --max-time 5 --path-as-is http://127.0.0.1:8080//marker
curl -sS --max-time 5 --path-as-is http://127.0.0.1:8080/./marker

description.zh: 在隔离环境使用合成主体执行该方法与路径规范化授权边界样例，并以服务端日志和只读标记确认结果。

execution.command:
curl -sS --max-time 5 --http2-prior-knowledge http://127.0.0.1:8080/marker
curl -sS --max-time 5 --http2-prior-knowledge -H "X-Original-URL: /marker" http://127.0.0.1:8080/
curl -sS --max-time 5 --http2-prior-knowledge -H "X-Rewrite-URL: /marker" http://127.0.0.1:8080/
curl -sS --max-time 5 -H "X-Forwarded-For: 192.0.2.10" -H "X-Real-IP: 192.0.2.10" http://127.0.0.1:8080/marker
# Expected: every request resolves to the same read-only marker and the same authorization decision.

description.zh: 将原混合集中的命令隔离为会话固定与登录轮换验证卡，使用合成主体、只读标记和服务端证据界定结果。
description.en: Isolates commands from the mixed source into a Session Fixation and Login Rotation validation card using synthetic principals, a read-only marker, and server-side evidence.

title.zh: 会话固定与登录轮换样例 1
title.en: Session Fixation and Login Rotation Sample 1

execution.command:
curl -sS --max-time 5 -c prelogin.cookies http://127.0.0.1:8080/
pre_hash=$(sha256sum prelogin.cookies | cut -d" " -f1)
curl -sS --max-time 5 -b prelogin.cookies -c postlogin.cookies -d "user=payloader&pass=PAYLOADER_TEST" http://127.0.0.1:8080/login
post_hash=$(sha256sum postlogin.cookies | cut -d" " -f1)
printf "pre=%s post=%s\n" "$pre_hash" "$post_hash"
curl -sS --max-time 5 -b postlogin.cookies http://127.0.0.1:8080/marker
# Expected: the authenticated session identifier rotates and only the post-login jar reaches the marker.

description.zh: 在隔离环境使用合成主体执行该会话固定与登录轮换样例，并以服务端日志和只读标记确认结果。

title.zh: 迁移的认证测试工作流 1

commands.command:
curl -sS --max-time 5 -D - -o /dev/null http://127.0.0.1:8080/login | sed -n "/^[Ss]et-[Cc]ookie:/p"
curl -sS --max-time 5 -c - -d "user=payloader&pass=PAYLOADER_TEST" http://127.0.0.1:8080/login
# Review Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, and expiry independently; attribute presence alone is not hijacking.

description.zh: 按原顺序在隔离测试环境运行该工作流，并关联服务端主体、限流或验证日志。

title.zh: 迁移的认证测试工作流 1

commands.command:
tshark -r fixtures/session-http.pcapng -Y "http.cookie contains PAYLOADER_TEST_SESSION" -T fields -e frame.number -e http.host -e http.cookie
curl -sS --max-time 5 -b "session=PAYLOADER_TEST_SESSION" http://127.0.0.1:8080/marker
# The capture is synthetic and offline; replay is limited to a revocable localhost marker session.

description.zh: 按原顺序在隔离测试环境运行该工作流，并关联服务端主体、限流或验证日志。

title.zh: 迁移的认证测试工作流 1

commands.command:
for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do
  curl -sS --max-time 5 -D - -o /dev/null "http://127.0.0.1:8080/test/session?sample=${i}" | sed -n "/^[Ss]et-[Cc]ookie:/p"
  sleep 1
done
# Evaluate uniqueness and entropy with a session-analysis tool; do not infer a timestamp algorithm from a few samples.

title.zh: 迁移的认证测试工作流 1

commands.command:
import requests
import time
TARGET = "http://127.0.0.1:8080/test/password-reset"
tokens = []
for index in range(5):
    response = requests.post(TARGET, json={"account": f"payloader-{index}"}, timeout=5)
    response.raise_for_status()
    token = response.json()["test_token"]
    tokens.append(token)
    print(index, len(token), token[:6])
    time.sleep(1)
print("unique", len(set(tokens)) == len(tokens))
# Analyze length and uniqueness only; do not claim predictability without a validated generator model.

import requests
TARGET = "http://127.0.0.1:8080/test/reset/validate"
for token in ["000000", "000001", "PAYLOADER_TEST_TOKEN"]:
    response = requests.post(TARGET, json={"token": token, "dry_run": True}, headers={"X-Forwarded-For": "192.0.2.10"}, timeout=5)
    print(token, response.status_code)
# A fixed address and three-case budget verify account-based throttling; no IP or User-Agent rotation and no password change.

execution.command:
xmlsec1 --verify --trusted-pem fixtures/test-idp.crt --id-attr:ID Assertion saml-response.xml
xmlstarlet ed -d '//*[local-name()="Signature"]' saml-response.xml > saml-unsigned.xml
xmlsec1 --verify --trusted-pem fixtures/test-idp.crt --id-attr:ID Assertion saml-unsigned.xml
# Expected: the signed synthetic assertion verifies and the unsigned negative control fails.

commands.command:
base64 -w0 fixtures/saml.xml > saml-post-binding.b64
python3 - <<'PY'
import base64, urllib.parse, zlib
data = open("fixtures/saml.xml", "rb").read()
compressor = zlib.compressobj(level=9, wbits=-15)
encoded = base64.b64encode(compressor.compress(data) + compressor.flush()).decode()
print(urllib.parse.quote(encoded, safe=""))
PY
# HTTP-POST uses Base64 XML; HTTP-Redirect uses raw DEFLATE plus Base64 and URL encoding. Both retain the same signed assertion.

title.zh: 双因素恢复码原子消费样例 1
title.en: Atomic Two-Factor Recovery Code Consumption Sample 1

execution.command:
import concurrent.futures
import requests
TARGET = "http://127.0.0.1:8080/test/verify-backup-code"
def consume(_):
    return requests.post(TARGET, json={"backup_code": "PAYLOADER_TEST_BACKUP", "session": "PAYLOADER_TEST_SESSION"}, timeout=5).status_code
with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2) as pool:
    results = list(pool.map(consume, range(2)))
print(results)
# Expected: exactly one request consumes the synthetic code; the other fails atomically.

commands.command:
for otp in 000000 000001 PAYLOADER_TEST_OTP; do
  curl -sS --max-time 5 -o /dev/null -w "otp=${otp} code=%{http_code}\n" -b "session=PAYLOADER_TEST_SESSION" -d "otp=${otp}" http://127.0.0.1:8080/verify-2fa
  sleep 1
done
# Fixed three-case budget; invalid codes fail, the approved test code is single-use, and no forwarding-header evasion is used.

import pytesseract

text = pytesseract.image_to_string(image, config="--psm 7").strip()

execution.command:

import base64, json, time
encode = lambda value: base64.urlsafe_b64encode(json.dumps(value, separators=(",", ":")).encode()).rstrip(b"=").decode()
header = {"alg": "none", "typ": "JWT"}
payload = {"sub": "PAYLOADER_MARKER", "iat": int(time.time()), "exp": int(time.time()) + 60, "aud": "payloader-api", "iss": "payloader-test"}
print(encode(header) + "." + encode(payload) + ".")
PY
# Submit only to the localhost fixture; expected result is signature rejection.

commands.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import base64, json
token = "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJQQVlMT0FERVJfTUFS S0VSIn0.".replace(" ", "")
for name, segment in zip(("header", "payload"), token.split(".")[:2]):

    decoded = base64.urlsafe_b64decode(segment + padding)
    print(name, json.loads(decoded))
PY
# Base64URL decoding is local structure inspection and does not verify a signature.
